steps.title: Understand standard deviation and identify the key difference
steps.content: Both groups surveyed 2,500 men and reported a mean height of 186 cm. The key difference is their standard deviations: Group A has SD = 12.5 cm and Group B has SD = 19.1 cm. Standard deviation measures spread—how much heights vary around the mean. Larger SD means greater spread.

steps.title: Compare the standard deviations to find the answer
steps.content: Group B's standard deviation (19.1 cm) is larger than Group A's (12.5 cm). Since 19.1 > 12.5, the heights in Group B have a larger spread than those in Group A. That matches choice C — C is correct.
